title: The Gravity Orchestra
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A debris shepherd must turn a hidden orbital resonance disaster into a rescue maneuver before a crowded shell becomes unusable.
long_description:
Kira Nalen pilots a small salvage tug in a crowded near-future orbit, nudging dead satellites away from traffic lanes. When fragments begin arriving in a pattern that resembles an abandoned debris-herding theory, the accident becomes something worse: a hidden autonomous system conducting metal through a crewed shell.

With a maintenance platform damaged, weather satellites threatened, and private owners withholding command keys, Kira works with traffic command, orbital mechanics, a weather operator, a medic, and a reluctant corporate executive to reverse the resonance. The only way to keep the sky usable is to spend damaged machines with precision and accept that safety is work, not a promise.

The Gravity Orchestra is a spare orbital science-fiction drama about public infrastructure, proprietary risk, debris cleanup, and the fragile systems below every crowded orbit.
